ground_truth.title: Designing The Future: 7 Interior Design Trends For 2025
ground_truth.author: beatriz.pinto@hommes.studio
ground_truth.main_content: Designing The Future: 7 Interior Design Trends For 2025 Skip to content All Products Seating Tables Storage Mirrors Beds Lighting Outdoor New Fashion Designing the Future: A Deep Dive into 7 Interior Design Trends for 2025 2025•01•08 Book a Call --> Trade Program --> Trade Program Explore the top interior design trends for 2025, from earthy colors to sustainability, and transform your space with the latest design innovations. Interior design is more than just aesthetics — it’s about creating spaces that resonate with the times while offering personal comfort and style. As we step into 2025, the latest interior design trends showcase a blend of sustainability, nostalgia, and bold creativity. Whether you’re a designer, an architect, or simply passionate about crafting beautiful spaces, this year’s interior design trends provide endless inspiration. Leading platforms like 1stdibs, known for its curated collection of luxury furniture, vintage pieces, and contemporary design, offer a glimpse into the most sought-after styles of the year. As you explore what’s on the horizon, you’ll notice how these trends are reshaping how we design and live in our spaces. Let’s delve into the top interior design trends for 2025, what’s fading out, and how to incorporate these elements into your designs. In with the New: The Defining Interior Design Trends of 2025 1. Warm and Earthy Colors: Comfort in Every Corner Leading the charge in interior design trends for 2025, earthy colors are transforming homes into sanctuaries of warmth and serenity. Chocolate brown takes center stage, accompanied by deep reds, burgundies, and natural tones like sage green and soft blues. These hues evoke calmness while keeping spaces grounded and inviting, offering a more intimate and organic atmosphere . Expanded Impact: Earthy colors are more than just an aesthetic choice: they reflect a growing desire for spaces that connect to the natural world. By incorporating natural materials like wood, stone, and linen alongside these tones, the interior design trends become a reflection of a more sustainable and mindful approach to living. Incorporation Tip: Think about a chocolate-brown accent wall paired with emerald green furniture. Alternatively, use burgundy throw pillows or a sage-toned rug to subtly infuse these colors. Earthy-colored wallpapers and textured fabrics are also perfect for creating depth and richness in your designs. Becket Bench Bench € 7.890,00 Kongo Armchair Armchair Get Price Cadiz Bench Bench € 4.500,00 Annali Console Table Console Table € 8.600,00 2. Nostalgic Revival: The ’20s and ’30s Return Vintage styles from the 1920s and 1930s are making a bold comeback in 2025. Art Deco ’s geometric elegance and Bauhaus minimalism are particularly popular, as they bring a sense of craftsmanship and character to modern interiors. These interior design trends emphasize a perfect blend of form and function , combining intricate detailing with modern practicality. Expanded Impact: The return to vintage styles taps into a collective desire for something timeless and luxurious. Art Deco lighting , sculptural furniture, and sleek materials like lacquered wood and polished metal can transport spaces to an era that values both craftsmanship and opulence. Incorporation Tip: Combine vintage-inspired lighting with sleek, contemporary furniture. A Bauhaus armchair or an Art Deco chandelier can serve as a conversation-starting centerpiece in your living room or dining area. 3. Maximalism and Bold Patterns Take Center Stage The era of minimalism is fading, and maximalism is taking over. Bold prints, vibrant colors, and eclectic furniture combinations define this year’s interior design trends. This trend celebrates personal expression through bold patterns, rich textures , and layers of eclecticism, bringing joy and energy to every corner of your home. Expanded Impact: Maximalism encourages you to tell your story through design — each piece, from animal prints to geometric patterns , has meaning and significance. The boldness of maximalism creates spaces that feel alive, reflecting the personality and tastes of those who inhabit them. Incorporation Tip: Layer patterns through textiles and wall treatments. A leopard-print ottoman or a floral wallpapered accent wall adds life and personality to any room. Mix bold and subtle pieces to prevent overwhelming the space while embracing the joyful spirit of maximalism . 4. Curved and Organic Shapes: Soft Elegance Curves are dominating furniture and architectural design in 2025. From rounded sofas to circular coffee tables, these organic forms introduce softness and elegance , enhancing both comfort and style. These interior design trends create a fluid, harmonious atmosphere that invites relaxation and balance. Expanded Impact: Curved furniture can soften harsh angles and create a sense of calm . The popularity of organic shapes in interior design trends suggests a return to spaces that are more inviting and less rigid. Curves also help with space planning, making areas feel more expansive and less cluttered. Incorporation Tip: Opt for a curved sofa to anchor your living room or use round mirrors to soften the sharp lines of other furniture. Circular rugs or arched doorways are subtle yet effective ways to incorporate these organic shapes into your designs. Madison Daybed Daybed Get Price Giulia Sofa Sofa € 11.500,00 Lunarys Center Table Fior di Bosco Center Table € 6.100,00 Duna Center Table Center Table Get Price 5. Classic Glass Lighting: Timeless Elegance Lighting continues to be a transformative element in interior design. Murano glass pendants and chandeliers are among the most sought-after fixtures for 2025. These interior design trends combine artistry with functionality, offering an elegant, vintage-inspired lighting option that adds an element of timeless sophistication to any space. Expanded Impact: Glass lighting brings both form and function, offering light that is soft, diffused, and ethereal. As we move further into 2025, incorporating glass lighting is a way to infuse luxury and a touch of the past into contemporary interiors. Murano glass and similar artisan-crafted pieces are an investment in quality and beauty. Incorporation Tip: Install Murano glass pendants above a kitchen island or use a statement chandelier in the dining room for a touch of vintage-inspired sophistication. Glass lighting can also serve as a focal point, drawing the eye and transforming the ambiance of a room.
